prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with a very dark or black frame, with no clearly visible objects, people, or text.

By the final sampled frame, the image has changed to a close-up of a person’s face, centered in the frame. Only parts of the face are visible because a large translucent Burger King logo is superimposed over it. The person appears to have dark hair, and their eyes are visible above/through the overlay, looking forward. The logo dominates the center of the image: it contains the words “BURGER KING” in large rounded pink/magenta letters, set inside a stylized burger-shaped emblem with orange and white areas. The background behind the person is plain and softly lit, and the image has black vertical borders at the sides.

No other actions or scene changes are visible in the sampled frames.
